objective: Build a real-time streaming pipeline using Dataflow (Apache Beam) to consume user clickstream events (page views, clicks, session activity) from a Confluent Kafka cluster and write them to BigQuery for live user behaviour dashboards and funnel analysis, with events available within 60 seconds of source publish and no data loss.
business_context: User clickstream events are currently published to a Confluent Kafka cluster but are unavailable for analytics. Delivering these events into BigQuery within 60 seconds will power real-time Looker dashboards and funnel analysis, enabling live visibility into user behaviour across page views, clicks, and session activity.
functional_requirements: Consume events in real-time from 3 Confluent Kafka topics (clickstream.page_views, clickstream.user_events, clickstream.sessions) using Dataflow Streaming
Parse JSON event payloads and map fields to the corresponding BigQuery table schema
Deduplicate events on event_id within a 10-minute sliding window
Partition BigQuery target tables by event_timestamp (DATE)
Route unparseable or schema-invalid events to a dead-letter Kafka topic without interrupting the pipeline
Log pipeline run results to pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log

non_functional.scalability: Dataflow job must auto-scale between 2 and 20 workers based on load
non_functional.latency: Events must be visible in BigQuery within 60 seconds of Kafka publish timestamp
non_functional.sla: Zero data loss; event count in Kafka must match BigQuery row count per hour; dead-letter queue must capture all malformed events without pipeline failure

security.access_controls: Confluent Kafka authentication via SASL/PLAIN API key

assumptions: BigQuery target project is verizon-data as stated in connection details
Audit log table pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log is expected to reside within the verizon-data BigQuery project
SASL/PLAIN API key credentials for Confluent Kafka will be securely injected at deployment time and are not embedded in pipeline code
Dead-letter destination is a separate Kafka topic (not a BigQuery table) based on the term dead-letter topic used in requirements
inferred_assumptions: Deployment environment is assumed to be production given the real-time Looker dashboard requirement and zero-data-loss SLA
Clickstream event data may contain user-identifiable behavioural data, implying potential PII sensitivity not explicitly declared in the ticket
acceptance_criteria: End-to-end latency < 60 seconds measured from Kafka publish timestamp to BigQuery insert confirmation
Zero data loss — event count in Kafka matches BigQuery row count on a per-hour basis
Dead-letter topic captures all malformed or schema-invalid events without dropping or stalling the pipeline
Dataflow job auto-scales between 2 and 20 workers in response to load changes
event_id is not null and is unique within any 10-minute deduplication window
event_timestamp falls within ±5 minutes of wall clock time at ingestion
All pipeline run results are logged to pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log
expected_outputs: Real-time Dataflow streaming pipeline consuming from Confluent Kafka (3 topics) and writing to BigQuery
Populated BigQuery dataset verizon_clickstream_deah (project: verizon-data) with tables page_views, user_events, and sessions, each partitioned by event_timestamp (DATE)
Dead-letter Kafka topic capturing unparseable and schema-invalid events for downstream inspection
Deduplication mechanism enforcing event_id uniqueness within a 10-minute window
Pipeline audit log records written to pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log for observability
BigQuery data available to real-time Looker dashboards and funnel analysis workloads
raw_text:
Ticket: SCRUM-117
Summary: Build Real-time Clickstream Streaming Pipeline from Kafka to BigQuery — New Development
Status: To Do
Priority: Medium
Assignee: Unassigned
Description:
Project Name: Clickstream Analytics Platform Requirement Type: New Development Stakeholder: John Objective User clickstream events (page views, clicks, session activity) are published to a Confluent Kafka cluster but are unavailable for analytics. Build a real-time streaming pipeline using Dataflow (Apache Beam) to consume events from Kafka topics and write them to BigQuery for live user behaviour dashboards and funnel analysis. Expected Outcome: Clickstream events available in BigQuery verizon_clickstream_deah dataset within 60 seconds of source event with no data loss, supporting real-time Looker dashboards. Connections and DB Details Source: Confluent Kafka, Bootstrap Server: pkc-abc12.us-central1.gcp.confluent.cloud:9092, Topics: clickstream.page_views, clickstream.user_events, clickstream.sessions, Auth: SASL/PLAIN API key Target: BigQuery project verizon-data, Dataset: verizon_clickstream_deah, Tables: page_views, user_events, sessions Functional Requirements Consume events from 3 Kafka topics in real-time using Dataflow Streaming Parse JSON event payloads and map to BigQuery schema Deduplicate events on event_id within a 10-minute window Partition BigQuery tables by event_timestamp (DATE) Dead-letter topic for unparseable or schema-invalid events Non-Functional Requirements Latency: Events visible in BigQuery within 60 seconds of publish Throughput: ~5,000 events/second peak Data volume: ~50GB/day Acceptance Criteria Success Conditions: End-to-end latency < 60 seconds measured from Kafka publish to BigQuery insert Zero data loss — event count in Kafka matches BigQuery row count per hour Dead-letter queue captures malformed events without dropping pipeline Dataflow job auto-scales between 2–20 workers based on load Validation Rules: event_id must be not null and unique within 10-minute dedup window event_timestamp must be within ±5 minutes of wall clock time Results logged to pipeline_audit.pipeline_run_log
